thesis_headline: Alphabet at a reasonable valuation — Francis Chou's bet on the world's most profitable information monopoly with $73B in annual free cash flow
thesis_summary: Francis Chou's 11% allocation to Alphabet GOOG shares reflects his deep-value approach to owning high-quality businesses. Chou, who manages from Windsor, Ontario and is known for his patient, contrarian style, sees Alphabet as one of the few large-cap companies that qualifies as genuinely cheap on a cash-flow basis. Google Search's 90%+ market share, YouTube's 2.5B MAU, and Google Cloud's 30%+ growth provide a diversified and growing revenue base. At 28x earnings with $73B in free cash flow, Chou sees a wide margin of safety in a business that the market periodically discounts on AI disruption fears.
thesis_bullets: Alphabet owns the world's most profitable information monopoly — Google Search with 90%+ global market share
FY2025: $403B revenue, $132B net income, $73B free cash flow — extraordinary profitability at scale
At 28x earnings, Alphabet trades at a reasonable multiple for 15%+ growth with dominant competitive positions
Chou's value approach: the AI disruption narrative periodically depresses the stock, creating buying opportunities
DeepMind/Gemini AI assets and Google Cloud growth provide optionality that is undervalued in the current price
business_bullets: Alphabet is the parent company of Google, operating Search (90%+ share), YouTube (2.5B MAU), Google Cloud (#3), and Android (3B+ devices)
Revenue is primarily from digital advertising (~77%), with Google Cloud and other bets providing diversification
The company operates DeepMind, a leading AI research lab, with Gemini models integrated across products
FY2025: $403B revenue (+15%), $132B net income (+32%), $73B free cash flow, 32% operating margin
why_this_price: Chou's value framework looks at normalized free cash flow relative to market cap. At $73B FCF on a ~$2.2T market cap, Alphabet yields roughly 3.3% in free cash flow before growth. With 15%+ revenue growth and expanding margins, the total return potential is attractive. The market periodically prices in AI disruption from ChatGPT, but Chou sees Google as the leading AI company, not a victim of AI disruption.
investor_in_their_own_words: Francis Chou: 'I look for businesses trading at reasonable prices relative to their free cash flow power. Alphabet generates $73 billion in free cash flow — at this price, you're paying a very reasonable multiple for that.'
Francis Chou: 'Patience is the most important quality in value investing. I buy wonderful businesses when they're temporarily out of favor and hold them for years.'
Francis Chou: 'The market overreacts to narratives. The idea that Google will be disrupted by ChatGPT ignores the fact that Google is the world's leading AI company.'
moat_sources: Search monopoly — 90%+ global share with 25 years of data advantage
YouTube dominance — 2.5B MAU, irreplaceable creator ecosystem
Data flywheel — more users generate more data, improving AI models
risks: DOJ antitrust case potentially forcing structural remedies
Heavy AI capex ($50B+) with uncertain returns
catalysts: Gemini AI monetization across Search and Cloud
Google Cloud 30%+ growth and profitability inflection
company_brief: Alphabet Inc. (GOOG) is the parent company of Google, operating the world's dominant search engine (90%+ share), YouTube (2.5B MAU), Google Cloud (#3 cloud platform), and Android (3B+ devices). FY2025: $403B revenue (+15%), $132B net income, $73B free cash flow, 32% operating margin.
